finding: Per-row Readiness and Status are unlabelled colour dots with no legend — page status is not glanceable from the list.

evidence.quote: Readiness and Status columns show only coloured dots, no text labels
SEO readiness: Needs work ... Page status: Published

persona_rationale: I scan a list to know what is live and what is broken; a coloured dot tells me nothing without hovering, and I do not hover.

suggested_fix.current: Status encoded as an unlabelled coloured dot per row
suggested_fix.proposed: Coloured status pill with the word visible inline (Published / Draft / Needs work / Blocked)

finding: After saving a scheduled publish, the field renders empty and no 'Scheduled for {date}' status appears anywhere — a schedule cannot be visually confirmed.

evidence.quote: Scheduled publish field shows dd/mm/yyyy placeholder after save; no scheduled-for confirmation
the field re-rendered EMPTY and no "Scheduled for …" status appeared anywhere in the editor

persona_rationale: "Is this going live Monday?" is a question I must answer in five seconds; right now I cannot confirm a schedule even exists.

suggested_fix.current: Field empties after save, no confirmation
suggested_fix.proposed: Persistent 'Scheduled to publish {date}, {time}' banner in the publish panel plus a 'Scheduled' pill in the pages list

finding: Publish is gated behind readiness blockers revealed one at a time in internal jargon (e.g. 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL'), with no overview of what remains.

evidence.quote: Publish is gated behind a chain of readiness blockers that are revealed ONE AT A TIME in the status chip, in terse internal naming
draft · SEO Blocked / Fix SEO title with Publish disabled

persona_rationale: Publishing is the one action I might do myself; a guessing game of one-at-a-time error codes is unusable in a meeting.

suggested_fix.current: One cryptic blocker shown at a time in the status chip
suggested_fix.proposed: A single plain-language checklist of all remaining blockers, each with a jump-to-field link

finding: After confirming publish, the 'Changes published' toast fires but the confirm card stays open asking to publish again — success state is ambiguous.

evidence.quote: "Changes published." toast appears BUT the confirm card remains open asking to publish again ("This will replace the current live version…")
Confirm publish card with Cancel / Confirm publish buttons

persona_rationale: I can't tell if it worked or whether I'm about to double-publish; ambiguous success states erode trust instantly.

suggested_fix.current: Success toast fires but confirm card remains open
suggested_fix.proposed: On success, dismiss the confirm card and flip the status pill to 'Published'

finding: No 'View live page' link surfaces after publishing in the default panel — the live URL had to be typed manually.

evidence.quote: No "Open live page" link was visible after publishing in the default panel state — the live URL had to be typed manually

persona_rationale: After I publish, the only thing I want is to click through and see it live; making me type a URL is a dead end.

suggested_fix.current: No live-page link shown after publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a 'View live page →' link in the publish panel the moment status becomes Published

finding: Per-page readiness is a paragraph-dense report (8 signal rows plus 7 action items) with no one-line verdict.

evidence.quote: Search visibility Strong ... Search result Needs work ... Action Items Highest impact first
Readiness and publish panel dense with signal rows

persona_rationale: I will not read seven action items; I need the verdict in one line and the option to delegate the rest.

suggested_fix.proposed: One summary line ('Ready to publish' or '2 blockers, 3 suggestions') with the detail collapsed beneath it

finding: The disabled Publish button is not obviously disabled at a glance; the greyed state sits next to small blocker text.

evidence.quote: Publish button greyed with 'Fix SEO title' in small text beside it

persona_rationale: I shouldn't have to study the screen to learn why an action won't work; it should announce its own state.

suggested_fix.current: Subtly greyed Publish button with small blocker text
suggested_fix.proposed: Clearly disabled button with an inline 'Can't publish yet — 1 blocker' label beside it
